title: uinstopn
text: uinstopn Get a handle to an existing component instance. Explain this code long uinstopn (UHACT hEnv, UCHAR* instName, UHACT* phInst); Copy Parameters hEnv The environment handle as returned by uecreate or ueopen instName The name of the existing instance of which we want the handle phInst (Output) The instance handle Return Values See the "Call-In API Return Codes" topic. Use Library: ucall Description uinstopn can be used to get a handle to an existing instance. In Uniface V7, every instance could be uniquely identified by its name. So it was always possible to use uinstopn to get a handle to any instance. However, since then, instance names are no longer globally unique. For example, the instance name on the client might or might not match the instance name on the server. So it is not always possible to know the real name of a given instance. Note: Therefore, uinstopn is deprecated. If you need a handle to an existing instance, have the 4GL pass this handle to your 3GL code as a handle parameter. You can then directly use that handle to activate operations in that instance. Related Links Call-In API Return Codes Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise. Handles A handle is a reference to an object, such as a synchronous component instance, entity, occurrence, or field. Handles can be used to call operations on the referenced object, or to pass these objects as variables and parameters. uinstnew Create a new instance of a Uniface 4GL component.
